input.prompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

data.content:
Use the project-name convention you already have. In MSBuild, `$(MSBuildProjectName)` is available early enough for `Directory.Build.props`:

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.props -->
<Project>
  <!-- Properties shared by all projects -->
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- MTP properties – only for test projects -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

**Why this works:** `$(MSBuildProjectName)` is set before any imports, so the condition is evaluated correctly in `Directory.Build.props`.

**Alternative** — if your test projects don't follow a consistent naming convention, use `Directory.Build.targets` instead and condition on `IsTestProject` (which is set by the MSTest SDK/package):

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.targets -->
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'">
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

This works because `.targets` files are imported **after** the project file and its SDKs, so `IsTestProject` is already resolved. The `.EndsWith('.Tests')` approach in `.props` is simpler if your naming is consistent.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to conditionally apply MSBuild properties in a `Directory.Build.props` file so that certain properties (like `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and MTP runner properties) only apply to test projects, not production/library projects.

This is a knowledge question about MSBuild conditionals. The standard approach is to use an MSBuild condition that checks for the presence of a test-related package reference or a naming convention.

The most reliable approach for MSTest + Microsoft.Testing.Platform is to condition on the project file name or on whether the project references `Microsoft.Testing.Platform` / `MSTest`. Let me give a concise answer.


The cleanest way to handle this in `Directory.Build.props` is to use a naming convention check on the project file name, or to set an `IsTestProject` property explicitly in test projects and condition on that. MSTest actually has built-in support for this pattern, so I can leverage that mechanism. csproj file content, then `Directory.Build.targets` runs after. So if I want to condition on properties defined in the project file itself, I need to use `Directory.Build.targets` instead of `Directory.Build.props`. The simplest solution is either a naming convention like checking if the project name ends with `.Tests`, or using `Directory.Build.targets` with a condition on `IsTestProject` that's either set by the MSTest SDK or manually in test projects.
